item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is exactly what is anomalous. That it somehow can recognize devices with certain purpose.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't get what is anomalous about this! its a paper towel dispenser with a super battery that only powers paper towel dispensers.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wikidot do something about not being able to upvote comments.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What I really want to know:

If you use this to power a motorized revolving paper towel holder, will it stop working if you take the paper towel roll off the holder?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's not stupid stupid of even daveyoufool stupid, it's an entirely different level of stupid

It is true that only a select few humans may say that an entire level of stupidity was dedicated to them

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is one of my favorite articles on the site simply because of how stupid it is. It's not stupid stupid of even daveyoufool stupid, it's an entirely different level of stupid. And the fact that the foundation was able to recreate the anomaly suggests that this wasn't man-made and that this is just how the universe works. It's that last part that turns the article from "meh" to "good lord".
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-1441 works way too efficiently to be a Stuff product

as for SCP-1441, I downvoted and thought it boring. Then I realized… it's a cold fusion battery that only works on paper towel dispensers and burst out laughing and upvoted

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You know what? Yeah, the link isn't likely to be made official. It's kinda like trying to link 173 to AWCY?, but with a less-established SCP and GOI. I'll drop it. This also applies to the twin of my first post.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Only if the author Freudian thinks so too.

AndarielHalo wrote all The Stuff works as far as I know.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Does anyone else think that there should be retroactive link to these guys?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Given the idea of moving things to the -EX designation, I love the idea of discovering that while the generator is mundane, the OUTPUT is anomalous!
Paper towels for all

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

I REALLY like this skip. Poor researcher trying to harness fusion power, just gives up and requests a transfer. I just love that it refuses to do anything useful. It's like the deity/alien/demon that made this contraption is just laughing at whoever tries to use it.

Dispensing paper towels is the PERFECT job for this. The perfect amount of random, unnecessary and only infinitesimally useful.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh wow. This is brilliant. Cold fusion is a reality, so long as we're using it for the very most idiotic of purposes. I love it. So much.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh, yes…I love this. This is a good example of why main list articles don't have to be super-serious. This is goofy, but it's also a proper SCP at the same time. I heartily upvoted. Thanks for this. It made my day.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How many times and how real a problem is the frequent usage of letter P in SCPs?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I didn't see 1441 making ambrosia, either.

The coffee machine produces some pretty crazy stuff, does strange and unexplainable things like draining fluids from a human or producing "Pan galactic drinks".

This doesn't really do anything interesting. Granted, this has a cold fusion reactor. Ok. This is like a yoghurt that can be eaten only with a fork. When a spoon is used, it vanishes. Boom! That is some good anomalous stuff, now ain't it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted for being a clever idea, and for representing the 'Nati

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Just had a "days later" thought: this could still be put to non-paper towel uses, you just have to be clever about it.

Set up a paper-towel dispenser at a great height, rigged to dispense constantly. The stream of falling paper towels can be harnessed as if it was a very weak waterfall, turning a paddle-wheel to generate a trickle of electricity. The wheel isn't actually part of the mechanism drawing power, so the fusion cell shouldn't mind. Glue the ends of a stupidly long roll of paper towels together and you would even have an infinite dispensing loop that doesn't need periodic refilling.

I'm thinking you would need several thousand feet of paper towels and the wheel would have to be a marvel of engineering if it's going to respond to the force exerted by falling paper, and the whole thing would have to be set up in a space with little to no air-flow to prevent stray breezes from disrupting the trajectory. But if you do all that, you might just get enough power to charge your zune.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I apologise, it just really annoyed me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Lycan, this thread of conversation has been dead for two days, and Alivieina already addressed the issue. Leave it that way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "Fag" is a slur and, as it came into use, an insult. He is completely within his right to be offended, because the word is, in this context, by definition insulting. Just because you're free to do something doesn't mean you should or it isn't rude.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Didn't think my vote would be so controversial. Guess I could just leave it with no vote at all, would be easier.

that's (likely) voting on something other than the quality of an article.

I can't say no. The quality is great, it's enjoyable but also very silly. It's a kind of sillyness lacking any serious joke or backstory. That's why i said that it wouldn't be good as a -J too. You can be quite sure that a simple creepy article wouldn't be good in my opinion, I've red enough of them.

Another thing is I'm starting to regret joining the wiki so late, I get the impression that some of the people that are picking on newbs nowadays discovered the wiki a year or two later than me. Drawbacks of beeing a lazy lurker, irrelevant to the discussion but irritating.

Anyway, why do you even care? This is 60+ and my silly downvote won't change anything. I'm happy it's there though, a little token of my opinion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not really, I'm really enjoying many of those no-scare SCPs and even currently working on one now. It's just about this single one i just can't put it anywhere in my head.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Maybe it's a fundamental law of the universe that cold fusion only works with paper towels.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It doesn't matter if he doesn't like it he doesn't like it. The word has negative connotations. He asked you to not use it. Please respect that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Honestly, write an SCP that flays you alive and rubs you with salt (but only if you're a rapist) and that's fine, but when the focus is on paper towels suddenly that's silly because it's specific.

I love it. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Theater of the absurd, in SCP form. I love it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Free use of words man.

And you act like someone using the word "fag" is bad. If you're gay, and you're proud of it, how is it offensive for someone to call you a fag?

Your comment made no sense.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I get the absurd and I like how it's not really dangerous or creepy, just weird and random.

I am going to be blunt. Soulless is right when they say that it's probably better not to jump on peoples' reasons for downvoting, but I also think trends like this are a problem. If you enjoyed a piece and still downvoted it, that's (likely) voting on something other than the quality of an article. This is, I feel, is perfectly valid, but that does not mean it's any less of a stupid thing to do - in my opinion - and I want to discourage it.

Not really newbish, though, to be fair. Hell, certain members of staff have downvoted articles for what I feel are equally stupid reasons. So take that FWIW.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is quickly turning out to be a real problem.

I disagree immensely.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
By the same token, it should be acceptable to express an opinion about his opinion.

A vote-reason can be without malice and still be a terrible reason to downvote. Example: Excellent story; I downvoted because it used the letter P too much. If we can't debate the basis of an opinion, we can't ever eliminate the foolish ones. And that could be disasterous in the long run.

Not saying that applies here; "doesn't fit" seems like a fine and valid reason, to me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
He has a right to have any (non malicious) reason to downvote. Newbie or not. If the guy feels the scp isn't suited for the wiki for whatever (not scary, not anomalous, etc) then he can have that opinion.

Can we not be getting on people's cases for the reason of their downvotes?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sadly, I believe you are correct. This is quickly turning out to be a real problem.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm vaguely afraid that you might just be downvoting this because it's not scary.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: By that argument, doesn't the coffee machine not fit in the mainlist, either?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Best thing about this is how it's written— could be aggressively boring, but instead is aggressively weird… in what turns out to be the most mundane way imaginable. I love reading these kinds of entries as much as the really gutclenching ones. Great job being thorough but not too technical and funny without being too lolfoundation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It just doesn't fit here in my opinion. Sure, it's anomalous but as I said: It feels like part -J but wouldn't work as one for me too. I think that the fact, that I might like a piece of writing but still consider it unfit for the wiki, is quite understandable.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So you enjoyed reading it and you like it, but you downvoted it? I'm not sure I really understand.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I get the absurd and I like how it's not really dangerous or creepy, just weird and random. I laughed at the last note and I enjoyed reading this.

Downvote.

Sorry, it's just something inbetween a normal SCP and a -J that would not work as neither. Maybe I'm too newbish but it doesn't cut me at all.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'd appreciate it if you didn't use variants of that word, since A) this isn't 4chan and B) I'm gay.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Aaron Williams suggests it's the SCP equivalent of DRM.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wish the combination experiments worked — then every machine would have a paper towel dispenser on it so that the cold fusion would work…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What'd you just say about old(word removed at the reasonable request of Freudian)?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oldfagging it up in here

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I WONT CHANGE FOR YOU. PAPER TOWELS ARE MY LIFE.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Same here. Wiki, I also own a zune.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Either this, or the cold fusion battery just really values being part of the paper towel dispensing experience. I'm not sure which explanation I prefer.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The best part is that this means that someone, somewhere, invented a cold fusion battery for the express purpose of running a paper towel dispenser.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Not really my cup of tea, but upvoted as it's well done and I was amused by "Staff are reminded to replace rolls after full use for the convenience of others." I would probably concur with Kalinin that the end note could probably be cut back to just the sullen note that 1441 is being withdrawn from the -EX programme and its containment procedures revised - the table works very well to convey the utter futility the researchers feel as they work through every possible permutation.

(As a side note, what would happen if you rigged a generator to the other side of the motor? Guessing based on #10 it fails as soon as any load beyond rotating paper towels is placed on it, which to me would suggest sentience)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I used to have one. Then I used to have another. Now I have an iPod.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP for boring original shit, upvote.

Seriously, I didn't even want to comment at all, but your comment made me type all this out. Personally, I didn't like this SCP for the fact that it is borderline funny. I like SCPs that make you think. I like SCPs that are random. I like historical SCPs, and also, SCPs that are scary. I do not like -Js.

This SCP didn't do anything for me. I can easily imagine this being a -J, although there are way funnier Js than this one as it currently is. It is anomalous, but flat, I see nothing that would grip me. It is almost as if the author wanted to include both funny and serious in one SCP, resulting in a 50:50 mediocre SCP.

I am not saying this is bad. It could be better if it decided which way it wants to go. No vote

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Imagine if Deeds only had the ability to resole sneakers. Powerful yet very limited, and still unexplained.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This also remainds me a bit of 1330, as an SCP with no apparent sentience that still manages to fuck around with the Foundation. Inanimate objects 2- Foundation 0.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: 'SPC for SCARY! DOWNVOTE!'

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That is exactly what I pictured. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Cold fusion that inexplicably doesn't work in anything that isn't a paper towel dispenser doesn't seem anomalous to you?

And if it was an -EX, why wouldn't we have cold fusion?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think that this would work much better listed as a -EX rather than as a standard SCP. I'm not saying that wtf/mostly-harmless articles don't belong in the main list at all, but this just doesn't seem anomalous enough. No vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like how it seems to be mostly new people who "just don't like this" or "just don't get it."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "Cold fusion that works and isn't anomalous, and works on plain water. This it revolutionary! It will change the way that everyone lives!! It… it only works with paper-towel dispensers? … [EXPLETIVE REDACTED]!!!"
